finding_results.notes: The deck scene now establishes why the study is still unprocessed, why no deputy is physically on it in the moment, what gap Joel and Paul exploit, and what contamination/collusion cost they risk if Mallick catches them handling the RT envelope.
The RT reveal now lands Ray Toomey as a dead man in the back bedroom and makes Martin's 3 AM return feel like lived damage before the scene turns back to case logic.

Chapter 16 now presents one compressed, canonical RT document version: two sheets; written August 17, 2002; fire on August 11, 2002; cabin kitchen kerosene-heater cause; Ray Toomey in the back bedroom; faulty-heater ruling; Martin's 3 AM return to support the lie.

The safe contents now use a stable physical description: cash still present in the bank envelope, the RT item is a long manila business envelope, and the document inside is two sheets of yellow legal paper.
Vague 'something' hedges were replaced with specific nouns or sharper phrasing. Final chapter count is zero instances of 'something.'
The full RT document is no longer reproduced. Joel's read is conveyed through narrated summary, selective factual emphasis, and reaction rather than a second long on-page quotation.

The chapter's on-page RT document facts were aligned to the packet's Chapter 8 canonical version so the written artifact no longer presents a separate fire date, structure, cause, victim location, or ruling.
Final chapter word count is 5342 while preserving the heading contract.

finding_results.notes: Eight targeted interventions across both target spans (deck exchange and post-document argument). Deck exchange: (1) Paul's bare 'No.' at the safe-contents probe expanded into action beat plus precision response showing exactly what he did ('I saw the initials and I closed the safe'), breaking a 5-turn bare ladder to max 3. (2) Paul's 'Yeah.' to the house question given an action beat (hand over mouth), breaking a 5-turn ladder to max 3. (3) Paul's bare 'Sales look.' echo replaced with a characterized response showing his distinct read on Cliff ('The one he uses right before he makes an ask'), giving Paul a precision turn and Joel a confirming elaboration instead of transcript ping-pong. Post-document argument: (4) Action beat added to Paul's inference-chain turn ('tapped the edge of the desk once'), breaking a 3-turn bare run. (5) Action beat added to Joel's 'He had to.' response ('flattened the second sheet against the blotter'), breaking a 4-turn short-exchange run. (6) Joel's second bare 'Yes.' replaced with 'I know where this is going, Paul.' -- a record-correction move true to Joel's low repetition tolerance and cross-exam instinct. (7) Paul's bare 'Why.' and Joel's separate 'So that matters.' merged into Joel's single continuous argument ('So it matters. Because it gives Tommy room...'), eliminating a 3-turn bare monosyllabic ladder. (8) Paul's second 'Probably.' replaced with action beat plus 'I know what it risks.' -- Paul's stress-precision voice acknowledging the risk in his own terms rather than repeating a mechanical concession. All runs now at or below the 3-turn bare-exchange limit. Paul has 5+ turns showing precision or circling habit (lines 75, 79, 93, 111, 433). Joel has 4+ turns showing record-correction rather than generic monosyllabic prompting (lines 27, 43, 59, 357). No contraction-level violations introduced; uncontracted forms in revised lines are character-true (Paul's precision mode, Joel's legal emphasis). No snapshot-drift toward thesis-speech; revised lines preserve spoken roughness and character texture.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 16 was expanded into a full deduction chapter that earns the study access, normalizes the RT document to one canonical version, lands Ray Toomey and Martin's concealment as human consequence, and carries the chapter into Joel and Paul's one-hour decision split before Chapter 17.

Addressed the single MEDIUM finding (CH16_DIALOGUE_TRANSCRIPT_CADENCE) with eight surgical edits across both target spans. Broke transcript-ladder runs by embedding action beats, expanding bare monosyllabic turns into character-voiced responses, and merging redundant prompt/reply steps. Paul gained precision turns (safe-contents specificity, Cliff's sales-look characterization, risk acknowledgment under stress). Joel gained record-correction turns (cutting off Paul's evidence list, confirming with elaboration). No runs exceed 3 bare one-line prompt/reply paragraphs. Voice separation strengthened: Paul's responses now show his qualifier habit and insurance-precision stress mode; Joel's responses show his cross-exam instinct and low repetition tolerance. Minimal collateral churn -- all changes confined to dialogue cadence; no structural, continuity, or tonal changes.
